armed-conflicts.headline: New START Treaty Expires; Conflicts Persist in Gaza, Ukraine, DRC, Nigeria
armed-conflicts.summary: The New START Treaty, the last major nuclear arms control agreement between the US and Russia, expired on February 5, raising global security concerns. Intense hostilities continued in Gaza, resulting in dozens of civilian casualties and injuries. Ukraine's frontline areas saw civilian deaths and infrastructure damage, including an ambulance strike near Kharkiv, while violence in Ituri, DR Congo, led to civilian deaths and aid suspension. A terrorist attack on February 3 in Kwara State, Nigeria, reportedly killed over 100 people.

regions.name: Gaza Strip, Palestine
regions.detail: Intense hostilities in Gaza resulted in dozens of civilian casualties and injuries.

regions.detail: Frontline areas in Ukraine, including Donetsk, experienced civilian deaths and infrastructure damage.

regions.detail: An ambulance was struck near Kharkiv, causing civilian deaths and infrastructure damage.

human-rights.headline: Global Democracy Set Back 40 Years; US, China, Africa Face Violations
human-rights.summary: A Human Rights Watch report on February 5 indicated global democracy has regressed to 1985 levels, with nearly three-quarters of the world's population living under autocratic rule. The US faced criticism for immigration crackdowns and threats to voting rights, while UN experts urged China to provide urgent medical care to activist Yang Li. In South Sudan, a Doctors Without Borders hospital was hit by an airstrike. High levels of displacement and settler violence continued in the West Bank.

regions.name: Washington, D.C., United States
regions.detail: The US government in Washington D.C. faced criticism for immigration crackdowns and threats to voting rights.

regions.detail: UN experts urged China to provide urgent medical care to activist Yang Li.

regions.detail: A Doctors Without Borders hospital in Lankien was hit by an airstrike.

regions.detail: Ramallah and the West Bank continued to experience high levels of displacement and settler violence.

regions.name: Khartoum, Sudan
regions.detail: Khartoum, Sudan was noted as part of the wider African region experiencing human rights violations.

climate-events.headline: Europe and Africa Hit by Severe Floods; Record Heat in Tropics
climate-events.summary: February 5 saw widespread severe weather, with Portugal experiencing deadly floods from Storm Leonardo and Spain's Andalucia region receiving record rainfall. Floods also impacted Antibes, France, Corfu and Kefalonia in Greece, and prompted evacuations of over 100,000 people in four provinces of Morocco. Juan de Nova Island recorded its hottest February day in history, while Afghanistan faced unseasonably warm conditions. Experts warned that flawed economic models underprice climate risks, potentially leading to a global financial crash.

regions.detail: Kabul, Afghanistan, experienced unseasonably warm climate conditions.

public-health.headline: WHO Appeals for $1 Billion Amid 36 Global Health Emergencies
public-health.summary: The World Health Organization launched its 2026 global appeal for nearly $1 billion to address 36 emergencies worldwide, including 14 Grade 3 crises. Health facilities in Gaza faced critical needs for specialized treatment, while an ambulance was damaged during a humanitarian aid delivery in Ukraine. In the US, lead was found at unsafe levels in New Orleans playgrounds.

economic-crises.headline: Global Growth Stable Amid Trade Slowdown; Climate Risks & Geopolitical Tensions Loom
economic-crises.summary: Forecasts for global GDP growth in 2026 remained stable around 2.6-2.8%, but global trade decelerated in late 2025 with expectations of further decline in 2026 due to tariffs and policy uncertainty. The European Central Bank noted a spike in geopolitical uncertainty on February 4-5. Experts warned that underpriced climate risks could trigger a global financial crash, while rising oil prices due to Middle East tensions indicated early economic ripple effects.

regions.detail: The European Union's Central Bank noted a spike in geopolitical uncertainty.

regions.detail: Middle East tensions led to rising oil prices, indicating early economic ripple effects.

democratic-erosion.headline: HRW Report Highlights Global Democracy Decline; US Voting Rights Concerns
democratic-erosion.summary: A Human Rights Watch report released around February 5, 2026, indicated a significant setback for global democracy, with metrics returning to 1985 levels. The United States was specifically cited for undermining democratic pillars through immigration crackdowns and threats to voting rights, with calls for ICE agents at polling sites. Myanmar's military-backed party won a tightly controlled election that excluded major opposition groups.

regions.name: Washington, D.C., United States

regions.detail: A report indicated global democracy suffered a significant setback, returning to 1985 levels.

humanitarian.headline: Gaza Aid Threatened, Major Crises in Chad, CAR, South Sudan, Ukraine
humanitarian.summary: The humanitarian situation on February 5, 2026, was dire, with Israel's decision to revoke licenses for 37 international aid groups threatening essential services in Gaza. Major humanitarian response plans were launched for Chad and the Central African Republic to address severe crises and displacement. Aid operations faced direct threats, including an ambulance strike in Ukraine and a Doctors Without Borders hospital hit by an airstrike in South Sudan, where escalating conflict also disrupted aid. Food distribution was suspended in Ituri, DR Congo, due to fighting.
